title: Muscarella megalops
taxonomy: Muscarella megalops is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is a member of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae. The genus Muscarella is part of the tribe Epidendreae. Further classification places the genus within the subfamily Epidendroideae. More specifically, it belongs to the tribe Epidendreae, subtribe Pleurothallidinae, and subfamily Epidendroideae.

intro: Muscarella megalops is a species of orchid, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The health benefits of Muscarella megalops include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Muscarella megalops is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, often used to treat fever, cough, and sore throats, or as a topical application for skin irritations and wounds.
